category_title: First-grade students who struggle with independent writing and book selection
minimal_context: The student is at the workstation to self-select a book for daily independent reading time.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for independent reading. Please go to the workstation and select a book to read.

STUDENT: (Walks over to the workstation and immediately grabs a book off the shelf without looking at it.)

TUTOR: Are you sure that is the one you want?

STUDENT: (Drops the first book and grabs another random book from the bin, still not looking at the cover.)

TUTOR: Take your time and pick a book for today.

STUDENT: (Grabs a handful of books with no intentionality, not examining the covers or reading the first pages, and sits down with the pile.)

options.A: Ignoring all undesirable behaviors exhibited by the student at the workstation during the daily independent reading time.
options.B: Nodding or trying to maintain eye contact with the student at the workstation for as long as possible.
options.C: Mentioning reading or enjoying specific books available at the workstation during the daily independent reading time.
options.D: Quickly redirecting to the replacement behavior, delivering the preset consequence, and reinforcing the class's planned ignoring.
